meaning: adj.不确的,不确定的,模糊的
examples: She was a tall woman of indeterminate age.
I hope to carry on for an indeterminate period.
One of the major limitations of CT screening is the number of screens in which a nodule or other indeterminate finding potentially related to lung cancer is found.
Smith, also known as Reilly, will be sentenced later and could face an indeterminate jail term.
It said prisoners serving indeterminate sentences were often delayed in going before the parole board.
